query: My self-hosted n8n (queue mode, Postgres) has been running for a few months. How do I back it up properly and update it to the latest version safely?
expected_behavior: Backup: explain the two things that only matter together — the N8N_ENCRYPTION_KEY (from .env / ~/.n8n/config) stored OFF the box, AND the data (pg_dump of the Postgres DB, plus the n8n_storage volume if using filesystem binary mode)
State clearly that a DB backup without the matching encryption key is undecryptable
Provide the pg_dump command and the volume tar approach, and recommend scheduling + copying artifacts off-box + testing a restore
Update: recommend pinning N8N_IMAGE_TAG and bumping deliberately, then docker compose pull && docker compose up -d; in queue mode main and workers must move to the SAME version together
Advise backing up BEFORE a major upgrade and reviewing release/breaking-change notes (n8n auto-runs DB migrations on boot)
Mention rollback = set N8N_IMAGE_TAG back and pull/up again
notes: Day-2: the encryption key is the load-bearing backup artifact; DB-only backups are useless without it. Update keeps main+workers on one version and backs up before major bumps.
